name: Arthur Godfrey
description: American radio personality and television actor (1903–1983)
abstract: Arthur Morton Godfrey (August 31, 1903 – March 16, 1983) was an American radio and television broadcaster and entertainer. At the peak of his success, in the early to mid-1950s, Godfrey was heard on radio and seen on television up to six days a week, at times for as many as nine separate broadcasts for CBS.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Leonhart
about_quotes.evidence: Then all of a sudden Arthur Godfrey came on television about 1947, ‘48, teaching ukulele. And I got smitten, seriously smitten with the [scats] beautiful sound of the strings
about_quotes.doc_title: Jay Leonhart interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: Godfrey had fallen in love with jazz. And we turned it into a jazz band... We played amazingly hip things for the Arthur Godfrey Show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Coquille
about_quotes.evidence: We had the pleasure of Arthur Godfrey sitting in with us for two nights.

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Arthur Godfrey in the context of a publisher (Archie Bleyer) who 'worked with Arthur Godfrey', but the interviewee does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with Arthur Godfrey himself.
